codeReview.summary: The validation-cli-lifecycle feature implementation is comprehensive and well-structured. It successfully implements `maestro validate show|update` commands with all required functionality: milestone filtering, evidence persistence, waive-with-reason handling, retry-to-pending from failed/blocked states, and helpful transition error hints. The code follows project conventions, uses proper TypeScript types, and includes thorough test coverage.

summary: The validation-cli-lifecycle feature has been implemented correctly. The usecases (validation-lifecycle.usecase.ts) provide clean separation of concerns between showing assertions and updating them. The command handler (validation.command.ts) properly wires CLI arguments to usecases with JSON/text output support using the shared `resolveJsonFlag` pattern. All test coverage is present: 500+ lines of unit tests covering milestone filtering, evidence persistence, waive validation, retry paths, and transition errors; plus 500+ lines of integration tests validating CLI behavior. The implementation correctly fulfills VAL-VALIDATION-001 and VAL-VALIDATION-002 from the validation contract.
